prose:
Fukui reactivity indices for phenol (C6H6O)

Method provenance
Property: atom‐condensed Fukui functions f+ (electrophilic attack), f− (nucleophilic attack), and dual descriptor f(0) on the input geometry; headline value reported here is the largest electrophilic index f+.
Level of theory: DFT, B3LYP/def2-TZVP (tier “standard”), no density fitting (exact integrals) in PySCF 2.x via chemkit.
Geometry provenance: user-supplied /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/phenol/phenol.xyz; single-point charge analyses on this fixed geometry (no re-optimization).
Solvent / environment: gas phase (no implicit solvent).
Charge & multiplicity: neutral singlet (charge 0, multiplicity 1). For the N±1 states, chemkit used charge +1/–1 with doublet multiplicity (2).
Software/backend: chemkit fukui-reactivity skill → PySCF (SCF and Mulliken charges), internal version as in cli_invocation below.
Convergence & warnings: SCF converged for all three charge states. chemkit’s integrity checks report:
  • sum(f+) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)
  • sum(f−) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)
  • common header present
All checks passed (integrity.trustworthy = true); no engine errors.
Live log & files: the run streamed live output to its .out log (path given in the CLI header) and wrote the full JSON result to /home/erobles/chem-skills/.fidelity_scratch/tmpomphfdsj/live.json. The per-atom bar-chart PNG is at /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/phenol/argo_o3/20260706-185740_phenol_fukui_reactivity/agent_call_01_plot.png.

Headline result (most electrophilic site)
Atom 3 (C, index 2) has the largest f+ = 2.1028319.
The complete f+/f−/dual arrays are available in the result JSON for further analysis.
